hints.text: **Short** white plate + EU blue strip (Åland = short, fully white, no strip)
Outer lines **always solid white** (Sweden/Norway use dashes); middle lines varied — yellow lines being **phased out**
Bollard: long black-and-white, **white rectangle reflector front + two dots back**, **diagonal** black strip
Snow poles orange w/ a **thin white band near the top** (single band; Norway/Sweden = wider, lower, multiple)
Finnish looks **unique**: long words, **double vowels**, letters **ä ö** (Estonian similar but adds õ ü)
Street names end **-tie / -katu**; white street sign, black text
Road signs **yellow background**; red-border signs have a **thin yellow stroke** on the edge (unique to Finland)
Pedestrian signs have **5 stripes** (Sweden/Norway usually 4)
Directional signs = **solid blue bg, white text + arrows**; chevrons black w/ yellow arrows
Wooden poles w/ streetlight, **lamp double-bolted at the top** (Sweden double too but lower & angled)
Signposts often have **blue-and-white striped** markings
Dense **boreal forest** (pines + white-trunk **birches**); **Land of a Thousand Lakes** — water everywhere
Much Gen3 shot in **autumn** → distinct fall colours = good Finland tiebreaker vs other Nordics
Architecture modern/utilitarian (concrete apartments downtown); wooden-clad suburbs, some dark-red painted
**K-Market & S-Market** supermarkets operate only in Finland (recognisable logos)
'Shitcam' (Ari) low-quality camera: wide circular blur, **no sky halo**, brownish tint — Finland (& Spain) have the most
Regional roads **3–4 digit, first digit = region**, distributed **south→north** (1 south … 9 north)
**Swedish bilingual** signs on the south/west coast → don't guess inland; **Sámi** bilingual far north (alphabet uses **c**)
**Wind turbines** & most farmland concentrated along the **west/south coast**
**Lapland**: snow poles with **2 long reflectors** (2023+ coverage); small stunted spindly trees in the north
**Åland Islands**: monolingually **Swedish**, **red-tinted** pavement, **dashed** outer lines, short all-white plates
City transit: **Helsinki** green-yellow trams + blue/white buses; **Tampere** red trams ('NYSSE' buses); **Turku** yellow buses; **Jyväskylä** green; **Oulu** pink front
